basic_details.job_title: Haryana Police Constable PST Schedule 2026 Out - Check Date, Batch & Registration Number Range
basic_details.post_name: Male Constable (General Duty) and Male Constable (Government Railway Police)
basic_details.short_information: The Haryana Staff Selection Commission (HSSC) is conducting the Physical Screening Test for Constable (GD) and Constable (GRP) posts in continuation of an earlier public notice dated 11th June 2026. The commission has now released the batch-wise schedule for the next six days of testing, from 6th July to 11th July 2026.

important_dates.raw: Earlier Public Notice Issued: 11 June 2026 Latest Public Notice (Schedule for 6 -11 July) Issued: 2 July 2026 PST Conducted From: 6 July 2026 PST Conducted Till (as per this notice): 11 July 2026 | Candidates should keep checking the official HSSC website regularly, since the commission is expected to release schedules for further dates in similar batches as the process progresses.

physical_eligibility.details: While the notice primarily provides the schedule, candidates appearing for any HSSC physical test round are generally expected to carry the following. Candidates should also check their downloaded admit card, since it may list additional exact requirements: | PST admit card (downloaded from the official portal) Original photo ID proof (Aadhaar card, voter ID, or similar) PMT admit card / qualifying documents, if asked Passport-size photographs, if specified on the admit card Sportswear suitable for the physical test | For the confirmed list of documents, always refer to the instructions printed on your individual admit card.

important_instructions: HSSC has fixed strict entry cut-off times for each batch:
1st Batch: Report by 5:00 AM. No entry will be allowed after 5:30 AM. 2nd Batch: Report by 7:00 AM. No entry will be allowed after 7:30 AM.

Candidates are strongly advised to reach the test venue at least 30-45 minutes before their reporting time to avoid last-minute issues such as traffic delays, document verification queues, or gate closure.

Reach the venue well before the batch reporting time, since entry is closed strictly 30 minutes after the batch start time. Keep checking hssc.gov.in and adv012026.hryssc.com regularly for updates, since HSSC releases the schedule in phases. Carry a printed copy of the admit card along with valid original ID proof. Only candidates who have qualified the PMT round should report for the PST. Any change in venue, date, or batch will be communicated only through official HSSC notices.

_labels.download_here: Official Public Notice / Admit Card Portal

content_sections.heading: Haryana Police Constable PST Schedule 2026 - Overview
content_sections.text: The Haryana Staff Selection Commission (HSSC) is conducting the Physical Screening Test for Constable (GD) and Constable (GRP) posts in continuation of an earlier public notice dated 11th June 2026. The commission has now released the batch-wise schedule for the next six days of testing, from 6th July to 11th July 2026.

rows.details: Male Constable (General Duty) and Male Constable (Government Railway Police)
Two batches per day (5:00 AM and 7:00 AM)

content_sections.heading: Important Dates
content_sections.text: Earlier Public Notice Issued: 11 June 2026 Latest Public Notice (Schedule for 6 -11 July) Issued: 2 July 2026 PST Conducted From: 6 July 2026 PST Conducted Till (as per this notice): 11 July 2026 Candidates should keep checking the official HSSC website regularly, since the commission is expected to release schedules for further dates in similar batches as the process progresses.

content_sections.heading: PST Date and Batch-Wise Registration Number Range
content_sections.text: Candidates must match their registration number with the ranges given below to identify their reporting date and batch. Each day is split into a 1st batch (morning) and a 2nd batch (later morning). Find the row where your registration number falls within the listed range. That row tells you the exact date and batch you must report for the Physical Screening Test. If your number does not fall in any of these ranges, your schedule has not been released yet, and you should check the HSSC website again once a new notice is published.

content_sections.heading: Reporting Time and Entry Rules
content_sections.text: HSSC has fixed strict entry cut-off times for each batch: 1st Batch: Report by 5:00 AM. No entry will be allowed after 5:30 AM. 2nd Batch: Report by 7:00 AM. No entry will be allowed after 7:30 AM. Candidates are strongly advised to reach the test venue at least 30-45 minutes before their reporting time to avoid last-minute issues such as traffic delays, document verification queues, or gate closure.

content_sections.heading: Documents to Carry for PST
content_sections.text: While the notice primarily provides the schedule, candidates appearing for any HSSC physical test round are generally expected to carry the following. Candidates should also check their downloaded admit card, since it may list additional exact requirements: PST admit card (downloaded from the official portal) Original photo ID proof (Aadhaar card, voter ID, or similar) PMT admit card / qualifying documents, if asked Passport-size photographs, if specified on the admit card Sportswear suitable for the physical test For the confirmed list of documents, always refer to the instructions printed on your individual admit card.

content_sections.heading: Important Instructions for Candidates
content_sections.text: Reach the venue well before the batch reporting time, since entry is closed strictly 30 minutes after the batch start time. Keep checking hssc.gov.in and adv012026.hryssc.com regularly for updates, since HSSC releases the schedule in phases. Carry a printed copy of the admit card along with valid original ID proof. Only candidates who have qualified the PMT round should report for the PST. Any change in venue, date, or batch will be communicated only through official HSSC notices.

content_sections.heading: Important Links
content_sections.text: Official Public Notice / Admit Card Portal: Download Here Official HSSC Website: hssc.gov.in

faq.question: Q1. Is the Haryana Police Constable PST Schedule 2026 out?
faq.answer: Yes. HSSC released the schedule through a public notice dated 2 July 2026, covering PST dates from 6 July to 11 July 2026.

faq.question: Q2. Who is eligible to appear in this PST?
faq.answer: Only candidates who have qualified the Physical Measurement Test (PMT) under Advertisement No. 01/2026, Category No. 01 and Category No. 03, are eligible.

faq.question: Q3. What are the reporting batch timings for the PST?
faq.answer: There are two batches each day: the 1st batch reports at 5:00 AM with entry closing at 5:30 AM, and the 2nd batch reports at 7:00 AM with entry closing at 7:30 AM.

faq.question: Q4. How do I know my PST date and batch?
faq.answer: Match your registration number with the ranges given in the official schedule table. Each range corresponds to a specific date and batch.

faq.question: Q5. Where can I download my PST admit card?
faq.answer: Admit cards can be downloaded from the official advertisement portal, adv012026.hryssc.com.
